channel.title: The Painting on Shipwreck, the Rescue -- JMW Turner
channel.description: Recent content in The Painting on Shipwreck, the Rescue -- JMW Turner

item.title: Christie's Catalogue: J.M.W. Turner and James Orrock
item.description:
Kindly supplied by Dr. Colin B. Bailey, National Gallery of Canada.

Christie’s Stenciled Stock Number found on the stretcher of Shipwreck, the Rescue.

From Christie’s Archivist, Jeremy Rex-Parkes, 16 Mar 2001:

“Good Morning Mr. Setters; It was 73OAS and the sale was 4th June 1904, ‘The Highly Important Collection of Pictures and Watercolour Drawings,’ the property of James Orrock, owing to ill health and is ordered away from London, Lot 140, JMW Turner, RA, The Shipwreck 63 X 87 inches.

item.title: J.M.W. Turner's Signature and Inscriptions
item.description:
Beginning of the J.M.W. Turner story

Peter Paul Biró looking for fingerprints just before discovering the letters inscribed into the pier

Shipwreck, the Rescue — detail of the pier showing the location of the three blocks of paint that contain the three letters — J W T

This enlargement of the T shows that the paint is underneath the antique varnish. The angle of the photographic flash-lighting illuminates the varnish in all of the depressions of the surface, including the intaglio inscription of the letters (W and T in particular). By this method Paul Biró was adroitly able to image the letters quite clearly.

item.title: J.M.W. Turner's Style and Technique
item.description:
J.M.W. Turner’s style is described in a running discourse throughout this website. The following specifics are presented to help Turner advocates better understand the enigmatic character of this enigmatic master.

Shipwreck, the Rescue, detail: three men adrift on a spar, one with both arms raised high to hail the rescue boat, the other two draped and clinging for dear life. The loose brushwork is rendered as Sir George Beaumont would later disparage. The much echoed statement that he made while viewing other early Turner oil paintings, including Calais Pier:

item.title: Portrait of a Hero: Shipwreck, Slavers, and JMW Turner
item.description:
jmw Turner self portrait, Turner portrait, Madonna portrait, Shipwreck, JMW Portrait, shipwrec, madonna, turner slavers… J.M.W. Turner the source

jmw turner r. malcolm setters / graham settersjmw

“Great wooden vessel bears roughest of seas,

Tossed by the beckoning deep;

While souls near-free from mortal distress,

Still fight till their last precious breath.

Lost in their toil, despair and exhaustion,

Sheer fright their last dieing grip;

Anon an accomplice observer apart,

item.title: Setters' Appraisal
item.description:
J.M.W. Turner: the source

Robert & Graham Setters
British Columbia, Canada
Appraisal and Consulting

Paintings · Oriental Art · General Antiques

What One Should Look For When Acquiring Artwork

Quality · Originality · Support · Rarity · Condition

Quality might best be defined as the ability of an artwork to rest comfortably within its format. This takes skill and practice on the part of the artist, and it is the ‘master’ with the greatest ability to present a bondable unity for the viewer who gains ‘genius’ status.
